basic_details.job_title: Indian Army Officer Recruitment 2026 (Short Notice) - Apply Online
basic_details.post_name: Short Service Commission Officer (JAG Entry & NCC Special Entry)
basic_details.short_information: The Indian Army has released its recruitment notice for the 125th Short Service Commission (SSC) Officer Entries, inviting applications for the JAG Entry Scheme Course and the NCC Special Entry Scheme Course, both commencing April 2027. Unmarried men and women, including wards of Army personnel who lost their lives in battle, can apply under the NCC Special Entry route.

important_dates.raw: Notification Published: 10 July 2026 JAG Entry Scheme Course (Men & Women) - Apply Online: 17 July to 17 August 2026 NCC Special Entry Scheme Course (Men) - Apply Online: 20 July to 20 August 2026 NCC Special Entry Scheme Course (Women) - Apply Online: 21 July to 21 August 2026

application_fee.details: No application fee is applicable for any category of candidates - General, OBC, EWS, SC, ST, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, or Women. Recruitment in the Army is completely free of cost, and candidates are advised to be cautious of touts or agents.

age_limit.age_details: 19 to 25 years (as on 01-04-2027, for NCC Special Entry)
age_limit.details: Candidates must be between 19 and 25 years of age as on 1 April 2027. Age relaxation is applicable to eligible reserved categories as per government norms.

qualification.education_qualification: Law Graduate (JAG Entry) / Graduate with NCC 'C' Certificate (NCC Special Entry)

selection_process: Initial shortlisting of applications based on graduation percentage. Two-stage SSB Interview (Stage I and Stage II). Medical fitness examination. Final merit list preparation along with document verification.
Candidates holding a valid NCC 'C' Certificate are exempted from the written examination and proceed directly to the SSB Interview stage.
how_to_apply: Click on the 'Officer Entry Apply/Login' option on the homepage.
Register with your basic personal, contact, and academic details.
Log in and select the relevant course - JAG Entry Scheme Course or NCC Special Entry Scheme Course.
Fill in your detailed profile and upload the required documents - photograph, signature, degree marksheets, and NCC 'C' Certificate (for NCC entry).
Verify all details carefully before final submission and submit ahead of the applicable closing date.
Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Notification Published: 10 July 2026 JAG Entry Scheme Course (Men & Women) - Apply Online: 17 July to 17 August 2026 NCC Special Entry Scheme Course (Men) - Apply Online: 20 July to 20 August 2026 NCC Special Entry Scheme Course (Women) - Apply Online: 21 July to 21 August 2026

content_sections.heading: Indian Army Officer Entries 2026 - Course Details

rows.course: 125th SSC NCC Special Entry Scheme Course (Apr 2027)
rows.eligible_candidates: Men, including Wards of Battle Casualties

rows.course: 125th SSC NCC Special Entry Scheme Course (Apr 2027)
rows.eligible_candidates: Women, including Wards of Battle Casualties

content_sections.heading: Who Can Apply for Indian Army Officer Entries 2026?
content_sections.text: JAG Entry Scheme Course: Law Graduates (Men & Women) meeting the eligibility criteria prescribed by the Indian Army. NCC Special Entry Scheme Course (Men & Women): Graduate with a minimum of 50% aggregate marks, along with an NCC 'C' Certificate of 'B' grade or above. Wards of Army personnel killed in battle are also eligible under this scheme.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army NCC Special Entry - Age Limit
content_sections.text: Candidates must be between 19 and 25 years of age as on 1 April 2027 . Age relaxation is applicable to eligible reserved categories as per government norms.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army Officer Entries 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: No application fee is applicable for any category of candidates - General, OBC, EWS, SC, ST, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, or Women. Recruitment in the Army is completely free of cost, and candidates are advised to be cautious of touts or agents.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army NCC Special Entry - Selection Process
content_sections.text: Initial shortlisting of applications based on graduation percentage. Two-stage SSB Interview (Stage I and Stage II). Medical fitness examination. Final merit list preparation along with document verification. Candidates holding a valid NCC 'C' Certificate are exempted from the written examination and proceed directly to the SSB Interview stage.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Indian Army Officer Entries 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official website: www.joinindianarmy.nic.in. Click on the 'Officer Entry Apply/Login' option on the homepage. Register with your basic personal, contact, and academic details. Log in and select the relevant course - JAG Entry Scheme Course or NCC Special Entry Scheme Course. Fill in your detailed profile and upload the required documents - photograph, signature, degree marksheets, and NCC 'C' Certificate (for NCC entry). Verify all details carefully before final submission and submit ahead of the applicable closing date. Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Indian Army Officer Entries 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply is 17 August 2026 for the 125th SSC JAG Entry Scheme, 20 August 2026 for the NCC Special Entry (Men), and 21 August 2026 for the NCC Special Entry (Women) through the official Indian Army recruitment portal.

faq.question: Q2. Who is eligible for the Indian Army NCC Special Entry Scheme 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a graduate degree with at least 50% aggregate marks and possess a valid NCC 'C' Certificate with a minimum 'B' grade. Unmarried men and women, including eligible wards of battle casualties, can apply.

faq.question: Q3. What qualification is required for the Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants must be Law Graduates (LLB) who meet the eligibility conditions prescribed by the Indian Army. Both unmarried male and female candidates are eligible to apply for the 125th SSC JAG Entry Scheme Course.

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for Indian Army Officer Entries Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No. There is no application fee for any category, including General, OBC, EWS, SC, ST, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, and Women. The Indian Army recruitment process is completely free.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for the Indian Army NCC Special Entry 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes application shortlisting, a two-stage SSB Interview, medical examination, document verification, and final merit list preparation. Candidates with a valid NCC 'C' Certificate are exempt from the written examination.

faq.question: Q6. How can I apply online for Indian Army Officer Entries 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible candidates can apply online by visiting joinindianarmy.nic.in, registering through the Officer Entry Apply/Login section, selecting the desired course (JAG or NCC Special Entry), filling in the application form, uploading the required documents, and submitting it before the deadline.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for Indian Army Officer Entries 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply is 17 August 2026 for the 125th SSC JAG Entry Scheme, 20 August 2026 for the NCC Special Entry (Men), and 21 August 2026 for the NCC Special Entry (Women) through the official Indian Army recruitment portal.

faq.answer: Candidates must hold a graduate degree with at least 50% aggregate marks and possess a valid NCC 'C' Certificate with a minimum 'B' grade. Unmarried men and women, including eligible wards of battle casualties, can apply.

faq.answer: Applicants must be Law Graduates (LLB) who meet the eligibility conditions prescribed by the Indian Army. Both unmarried male and female candidates are eligible to apply for the 125th SSC JAG Entry Scheme Course.

faq.answer: No. There is no application fee for any category, including General, OBC, EWS, SC, ST, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, and Women. The Indian Army recruitment process is completely free.

faq.answer: The selection process includes application shortlisting, a two-stage SSB Interview, medical examination, document verification, and final merit list preparation. Candidates with a valid NCC 'C' Certificate are exempt from the written examination.
